'''''The Return of Tag & Bink: Special Edition''''' is an issue in the ''[[Star Wars: Tag & Bink]]'' series of comics, it was released [[March 29]], [[2006]]. It is the "special edition" of "[[The Revenge of Tag & Bink]]."

==Publisher's summary==
''Lock up your daughters and hide the Jawas, faithful readers, because Tag and Bink are BACK! Yea, though one might be hard-pressed to develop a sequel to the popular Tag & Bink Are Dead, fan-favorite writer Kevin Rubio and ''Star Wars'' stalwart Lucas Marangon have risen to the occasion!''

''And what an occasion it is. Fans jonesing for more side-splitting Star Wars shenanigans will thrill to the new misadventures of this hapless pair, members of the Rebel Alliance, who have somehow stumbled past the worst Imperial perils with their limbs intact. In ''The Return of Tag & Bink: Special Edition'', the duo set out to rescue a certain smuggler trapped in carbonite—with the help of Lando Calrissian! One thing leads to another, and they manage to subdue the deadliest bounty hunter in the galaxy on their way to infiltrating the Hutt palace. Star Wars fans know what comes next, and there will be guest appearances of favorite characters on the way to the Sarlacc pit, the Death Star, and Endor! This issue will conquer the world!''

==Plot summary==
{{Plot|comic}}

==Continuity==
A [[Wikipedia:Big Boy (restaurant)|Bob's Big Boy]] appears within [[Chalmun's Spaceport Cantina/Legends|Chalmun's Spaceport Cantina]], where one of the two refers to [[Lando Calrissian/Legends|Lando Calrissian]] as "Super Fly," a reference to the [[Wikipedia:Superfly (film)|movie of the same name]]. [[Tag Greenly|Tag Greenley]] also says "[[Wikipedia:Bob's your uncle|Bob's your uncle]]," a [[Wikipedia:British|British]] phrase that means "and there you have it" or "quickly."

In the scene of panic when [[Jabba Desilijic Tiure/Legends|Jabba]] is being choked, [[Wikipedia:Arthur Dent|Arthur Dent]] can be seen reading [[Wikipedia:The Hitchhiker's Guide to the Galaxy|''The Hitchhiker's Guide to the Galaxy'']]. Also, when Tag, Bink, and [[Manuel Both-Hanz]] are leaving [[Tatooine/Legends|Tatooine]], they pass a spinner from the movie [[Wikipedia:Blade Runner|''Blade Runner'']].

When [[Palpatine/Legends|Palpatine]] arrives on the [[Death Star II/Legends|Death Star II]], his honor guard includes two [[Battlestarwiki:Cylons (TOS)|Cylon Centurions]] from the [[Battlestarwiki:Battlestar Galactica (TOS)|original ''Battlestar Galactica'']]. [[Janus Greejatus/Legends|Janus Greejatus]]' comment that "you fight a rebellion with the [[Sith Lord/Legends|Sith Lord]] you have, not the one you wish you had" is a reference to [[Wikipedia:Donald Rumsfeld|Donald Rumsfeld's]] controversial statement, "you go to war with the Army you have, not the Army you want."

At the celebration on [[Endor/Legends|Endor]], [[Wikipedia:Yogi Bear|Yogi and Boo-Boo Bear]] can be seen dancing with the [[Ewok/Legends|Ewoks]]. In addition, two versions of [[Anakin Skywalker]]'s Force ghost, one young and one old, are looking at each other with confusion, a reference to [[George Lucas]] replacing [[Sebastian Shaw]] with [[Hayden Christensen]] for the Force ghost scene.
